title: Grapes in Midnight Dust
description: A luxurious and flavorful dish featuring grapes slow-cooked to perfection, with a touch of midnight dust for an added depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Use a natural sweetener if needed for the FODMAP diet

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Can substitute with bone broth or low-sodium vegetable broth

ingredients.name: Cinnamon Powder
ingredients.notes: Optional, for added depth of flavor

instructions: Soak the grapes in water for about 30 minutes to remove any bitterness and soften them slightly.
Drain the soaked grapes, reserving the soaking liquid. Add the grapes, reserved liquid, sugar, and cinnamon (if using) to a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat until the sugar is completely dissolved and the mixture starts to thicken, about 10 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for another 25-30 minutes or until the liquid has mostly evaporated and the grapes are tender but not falling apart when pierced with a fork.
Season with salt just before serving. Serve warm over a bed of fluffy, oat-based cereal for added texture.
